socialTrends.name: Reddit, Inc.
socialTrends.summary: Retail investors are highly optimistic about Reddit's advertising growth and its lucrative AI data-licensing partnerships with Google and OpenAI, which are generating solid recurring cash flows. While some users express concern over premium valuations (P/E of 53x), many see significant long-term potential as the platform's monetization efforts mature.

socialTrends.name: Tesla, Inc.
socialTrends.summary: Despite beating Q2 vehicle delivery expectations (480,126 delivered vs. ~406,000 estimated), the stock faced a classic 'sell-the-news' profit-taking drop of nearly 7.5%. Discussions focus on gross margins, heavy discounting, Elon Musk's Terafab semiconductor plan, and upcoming earnings on July 22.

socialTrends.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
socialTrends.summary: Fears of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) oversupply, triggered by rumors that SK Hynix is slowing expansion, sparked a major semiconductor selloff. Micron suffered a 5.5% drop on July 2. Retail traders are debating if this is a temporary dip or the bursting of the AI bubble.

socialTrends.name: Rocket Lab USA, Inc.
socialTrends.summary: Rocket Lab continues to be a favorite in retail space communities. Discussion is heavily focused on the upcoming first flight of the Neutron rocket, its recent Space Force record-setting responsive launch, and speculation over potential acquisitions.

socialTrends.name: UiPath Inc.
socialTrends.summary: Social mentions surged over 300% following the launch of 'Maestro Case'—an AI-native case management tool—and certification for government cloud clients in the UAE. Investors are hopeful its agentic automation strategy will reverse the stock's long-term slide.
